ucs.title: [LLM] Molerats (Gaza Cybergang) DropBook/SharpStage cloud-storage C2 — non-browser process talking to Dropbox/Google Drive/Facebook Graph APIs
ucs.description: Hunts the signature C2 channel of Molerats' Spark/SharpStage/DropBook/MoleNet families (Cybereason, Dec 2020) where dropper-installed implants beacon to api.dropbox.com, content.dropboxapi.com, drive.google.com or graph.facebook.com using stolen OAuth tokens hard-coded in the binary. We surface any process touching these APIs that is NOT a known browser/Office/sync client — Molerats' implants are written in .NET/C++ and call the REST endpoints directly with WinINet/WinHTTP, so the initiating process will be a renamed binary in %APPDATA% or %PUBLIC%, not chrome.exe.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("api.dropbox.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","*.dropboxusercontent.com","www.googleapis.com","oauth2.googleapis.com","graph.facebook.com","www.facebook.com")) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.process_path host
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| search NOT process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","googledrivesync.exe","backupandsync.exe","msedgewebview2.exe","slack.exe","code.exe","explorer.exe","safari.exe")
| where match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\(AppData|ProgramData|Public|Users\\\\Public|Temp|Downloads)\\\\")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - count
ucs.kql:
// Molerats DropBook / SharpStage / MoleNet — implant talks to cloud-storage REST APIs as C2
let _legit_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","googledrivesync.exe","backupandsync.exe","msedgewebview2.exe","slack.exe","code.exe","explorer.exe","powerpnt.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe"]);
let _c2_hosts = dynamic(["api.dropbox.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","www.googleapis.com","oauth2.googleapis.com","graph.facebook.com","www.facebook.com"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl in~ (_c2_hosts) or RemoteUrl endswith ".dropboxusercontent.com"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
// Molerats drops payloads to user-writable paths
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Process = InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ProcessCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ProcessSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Molerats Cloud-Storage API C2 (Dropbox / Google Drive / Facebook Graph)
id: a4d2f7e0-1b3a-4e2d-9d65-mole-ratsc2v1
status: experimental
description: Detects Molerats / Gaza Cybergang implants (Spark, SharpStage, DropBook, MoleNet) beaconing to cloud-storage REST APIs from a non-browser process running out of a user-writable directory.
references:
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/new-malware-arsenal-abusing-cloud-platforms-in-middle-east-espionage-campaign
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0021/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g0021
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dest:
    DestinationHostname:
      - 'api.dropbox.com'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'www.googleapis.com'
      - 'oauth2.googleapis.com'
      - 'graph.facebook.com'
  selection_dest_suffix:
    DestinationHostname|endswith: '.dropboxusercontent.com'
  filter_legit_clients:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\teams.exe'
      - '\dropbox.exe'
      - '\onedrive.exe'
      - '\msedgewebview2.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Downloads\'
  condition: (selection_dest or selection_dest_suffix) and selection_path and not filter_legit_clients
falsepositives:
  - Custom in-house tools that legitimately hit Dropbox/Google APIs from %ProgramData% — allowlist by SHA256.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Cybereason's 'Molerats in the Cloud' (Dec 2020) and Kaspersky's MoleRATs reporting (Apr 2019) explicitly document this group's reliance on Dropbox, Google Drive and Facebook as bidirectional C2 — embedded OAuth tokens fetch jobs and exfiltrate data via the public REST APIs. No other major APT cluster combines all three providers in a single toolset. Pinning the destination list to those exact host

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `AdditionalFields` is not a column of ImWebSession; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents, DeviceEvents, DeviceInfo, EmailEvents, IdentityLogonEvents, IdentityQueryEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents, CloudAppEvents, AlertEvidence, AADSignInEventsBeta, ASCDeviceEvents.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Molerats spearphishing chain — RAR/SFX decoy spawning scheduled-task persistence with politically-themed payload name
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Molerats initial-access chain (DustySky / Operation Molerats): Arabic-language spearphish (T1566.001) delivers a self-extracting WinRAR or .NET dropper that drops a PDF/DOCX decoy plus a payload (Pierogi, Spark, JhoneRAT, MoleNet) into %APPDATA% or %PUBLIC%, then registers a scheduled task or HKCU\Run key for persistence within seconds. We chain WinRAR.exe / 7zG.exe / Outlook-spawned executable → schtasks.exe /create OR registry Run-key write, on the same host within a 5-minute window.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winrar.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","outlook.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Rar*") (Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" Processes.process="*/create*") OR (Processes.process_name="reg.exe" Processes.process="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") Processes.process="*Registry::HKCU*Run*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where NOT match(user,"(?i)\\$$")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - count
ucs.kql:
// Molerats spearphish-to-persistence chain: SFX/RAR or Outlook-spawned dropper -> schtasks.exe or HKCU Run key within 5 min
let _persist_window = 5m;
let _droppers = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winrar.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","outlook.exe")
         or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\Rar", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\7z", @"\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Olk\Attachments")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              DropperFile = FileName, DropperPath = FolderPath, DropperSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperCmd = ProcessCommandLine, DropperParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
// Branch A — scheduled task persistence
let _sched = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
    | project SchedTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, SchedCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
// Branch B — HKCU Run key persistence
let _runkey = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project RegTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RegInitiator = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RegInitiatorPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath, RegKey = RegistryKey,
              RegValueName = RegistryValueName, RegValueData = RegistryValueData;
_droppers
| join kind=inner _sched on DeviceId
| where SchedTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + _persist_window)
| project Timestamp = SchedTime, DeviceName, AccountName, DropperParent, DropperFile, DropperPath, DropperSHA256, SchedCmd, PersistType = "ScheduledTask"
| union (
    _droppers
    | join kind=inner _runkey on DeviceId
    | where RegTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + _persist_window)
    | where RegInitiatorPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\")
    | project Timestamp = RegTime, DeviceName, AccountName, DropperParent, DropperFile = RegInitiator, DropperPath = RegInitiatorPath, DropperSHA256 = "", SchedCmd = strcat(RegKey, "\\", RegValueName, " = ", RegValueData), PersistType = "RunKey"
  )
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Molerats spearphish-to-persistence: dropper out of WinRAR / Outlook-temp -> schtasks.exe within 5 min (SecurityEvent 4688)
let _persist_window = 5m;
let _droppers = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (ParentProcessName endswith "\\winrar.exe" or ParentProcessName endswith "\\7zG.exe" or ParentProcessName endswith "\\outlook.exe")
         or ParentProcessName has_any ("\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Rar", "\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\7z", "\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Olk\\Attachments")
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("\\AppData\\", "\\ProgramData\\", "\\Users\\Public\\", "\\Temp\\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              DropperImage = NewProcessName, DropperCmd = CommandLine,
              DropperParent = ParentProcessName;
let _sched = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\schtasks.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "/create"
    | project SchedTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SchedCmd = CommandLine;
_droppers
| join kind=inner _sched on Computer, Account
| where SchedTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + _persist_window)
| project TimeGenerated = SchedTime, Computer, Account, DropperParent, DropperImage, DropperCmd, SchedCmd,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SchedTime, DropTime)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: ClearSky's DustySky reports and Kaspersky's 2019 MoleRATs writeup both anchor Molerats' kill chain on the WinRAR-SFX-with-decoy pattern (Operation Molerats) followed by very fast persistence registration — DustySky and the later Spark/Pierogi droppers either write a Run key or a Schtasks 'wininit'/'WindowsUpdate'-themed task within seconds of execution. Generic 'process from %APPDATA%' or 'schtask
